title: hotato simulation scenario, schema scenario.v1
description: A deterministic simulation scenario (Phase-2 design 2.1): the ground-truth a scripted caller holds, the caller's scripted turn sequence + behaviour, the environment, and a variation matrix. It is the deterministic INPUT side of a simulation -- there is no live agent and no scoring here. HONESTY INVARIANTS, structural: no `overall_score` (a scenario never scores anything); the caller only ever declares its OWN turns, never the agent's; a scenario is not a verdict. Malformed docs are rejected by hotato.scenario.validate_scenario_doc (ValueError, exit 2) BEFORE any use, mirroring conversation_test.validate_conversation_test_doc.

goal.description: What the caller is trying to achieve. `type` is the goal kind (e.g. get_refund); `target` names its object (e.g. order A-1001). Descriptive only -- never a success threshold.

facts.description: The ground-truth the caller HOLDS (e.g. {order_id: A-1001}). A valid simulation must preserve these -- the caller states its own truth, the simulator never alters it.

caller.description: The scripted caller: the ordered turns it speaks and how it behaves. The caller declares ONLY its own turns -- a scenario can never put words in the agent's mouth (invariant: the simulator does not solve the task for the agent).

script.description: The ordered caller turns. Each is a `say` (spoken unconditionally), optionally gated by a `when_agent_asks` label (spoken when the agent asks about that label) or sequenced by an `after` label.

environment.description: The declared rendering environment. All members optional.

noise.description: A named noise condition (e.g. clean, cafe). The declared perturbation a valid simulation must apply.

variation_matrix.description: The cross product `expand()` walks (locale x speaking_rate x noise x behavior x repetitions) into concrete runs, each with a deterministic per-run seed. An absent dimension collapses to the scenario's single declared value.

seed.description: The base seed. A seeded replay is byte-identical; different seeds vary only where the scenario allows (probabilistic backchannels). Never Math.random / time-based.

variables.description: OPTIONAL dynamic variables. Each key is an identifier and each value is a non-empty list of scalar values (strings or numbers). Every declared variable is an expansion axis: `expand()` crosses its values with the variation matrix, substituting `{name}` template references in the caller's `say` lines with each value. A `{name}` reference with no declared variable is refused up front (exit 2). Additive and gated -- a scenario without it expands byte-identically.

branches.description: OPTIONAL caller decision tree/DAG. `root` names the entry node; `nodes` maps a node name to its caller line(s) and successors. `expand()` enumerates EVERY root-to-leaf path deterministically and appends each path's lines to the base caller script as its own expansion cell (stamped with a `path` label). An edge to an undefined node (unknown node) or any cycle is refused up front (exit 2). Additive and gated -- a scenario without it expands byte-identically.

root.description: The entry node name; must be a key of `nodes`.

say.description: The caller line(s) this node speaks: a non-empty string, or a non-empty list of non-empty strings.

next.description: Successor node names. A node with no `next` (or an empty one) is a leaf that ends a path.

agent_mock.description: OPTIONAL deterministic MOCK agent (Phase-2 tool mocks + state sandbox; 1.3 item 9). Additive and gated -- a scenario without it renders byte-identically. Lets an OFFLINE simulation exercise the outcome/policy authorities end-to-end: `tools` render as tool_call spans (Authority 1, read by tool_result/tool_error), an optional `handoff`/`termination` render as their own spans, and `state` is a {resource: rows} post-call sandbox (Authority 2) served by a MockStateAdapter to state/state_change. A mock is never a real agent: the produced conversation stays origin=simulated and this evidence is labelled the simulator's, never a live agent's. It declares AGENT-side evidence only -- the caller script stays the only place caller words live.

turn.description: One scripted caller turn. `say` is the spoken text (required). `when_agent_asks` / `after` are optional label triggers; a turn may carry at most one.

behavior.description: How the caller behaves: its speaking rate, declared barge-in interruptions (fixed offsets), and probabilistic backchannels (the one seed-dependent variation point).

interruptions.description: Declared caller barge-ins at fixed offsets -- deterministic (same every seed). A valid simulation must render each declared interruption.

backchannels.description: Probabilistic short acknowledgements. The seeded PRNG decides each candidate -- probability 0 yields none; a fixed seed always fires the same set.
